channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Just Tech Fellowship 2027 by Social Science Research Council (SSRC): $60,000 Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers, Creatives, and Practitioners in Technology & Society
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Just Tech Fellowship 2027, offered by the Social Science Research Council (SSRC), is a prestigious and fully funded opportunity designed for individuals working at the intersection of technology and society. With an unrestricted award of up to $60,000, this fellowship empowers researchers, artists, technologists, and practitioners to explore how technology shapes public life and […]

The post Just Tech Fellowship 2027 by Social Science Research Council (SSRC): $60,000 Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers, Creatives, and Practitioners in Technology & Society appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Just Tech Fellowship 2027, offered by the Social Science Research Council (SSRC), is a prestigious and fully funded opportunity designed for individuals working at the intersection of technology and society. With an unrestricted award of up to $60,000, this fellowship empowers researchers, artists, technologists, and practitioners to explore how technology shapes public life and to develop innovative, impactful solutions.

This one-year fellowship program is not just about funding—it is a transformative platform that supports interdisciplinary collaboration, public engagement, and forward-thinking research that addresses some of the most pressing societal challenges driven by technological change.

What is the Just Tech Fellowship?

The Just Tech Fellowship is a flagship initiative of the Social Science Research Council aimed at supporting community-grounded, original, and socially impactful work that critically examines the role of technology in shaping society.

The fellowship welcomes applicants from diverse backgrounds, including:

  • Social scientists
  • Humanists
  • Artists
  • Journalists
  • Technologists
  • Community-based researchers
  • Public-interest practitioners

The program emphasizes interdisciplinary collaboration, ensuring that fellows contribute to a broader understanding of how digital systems influence governance, equity, public discourse, and everyday life.

What the Fellowship Offers

Selected fellows receive a comprehensive package of financial and professional support:

Financial Award

  • Up to $60,000 unrestricted funding to support:
    • Research projects
    • Creative work
    • Community-based initiatives

Professional Development

  • Monthly virtual cohort gatherings
  • Individualized mentorship support
  • Access to the Just Tech network

Collaboration Opportunities

  • One in-person workshop for fellows
  • Opportunities to collaborate across cohorts
  • Eligibility for additional seed funding for joint projects

Long-Term Engagement

  • Continued access to the Just Tech network beyond the fellowship year

Fellowship Duration

  • Fellowship Period: January 2027 – December 2027

The program spans one full year, allowing fellows to deeply engage with their projects while participating in structured activities and collaborative exchanges.

Fellowship Objectives

The Just Tech Fellowship aims to:

  1. Advance Critical Research
    • Support projects that interrogate how technology affects society
  2. Promote Public Engagement
    • Encourage fellows to share their work with broader audiences
  3. Foster Interdisciplinary Dialogue
    • Build bridges across fields and practices
  4. Support Innovative Thinking
    • Enable creative and experimental approaches to technology-related challenges

Areas of Focus

Applicants are encouraged to propose projects that engage with:

  • Digital technologies and public life
  • Technology governance and accountability
  • Artificial intelligence and ethics
  • Data justice and privacy
  • Social media and public discourse
  • Community-centered tech innovation
  • Technology and inequality

Projects may involve:

  • Research
  • Creative production
  • Policy engagement
  • Community collaboration

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a comprehensive application including:

1. Résumé/CV

  • Maximum of 2 pages

2. Personal Statement

  • Up to 1,000 words OR
  • Video statement (maximum 5 minutes)

3. Work Proposal

  • Written document (max 3,000 words) OR
  • Project deck (up to 10 slides)

The proposal must include:

  • Concept description
  • Engagement with technology
  • Approach and contribution
  • Feasibility plan
  • Field context
  • Public contribution

4. Work Samples

  • Two samples showcasing previous work
  • Brief descriptions explaining your role

5. AI Use Disclosure

  • Applicants may use AI tools for assistance, but submissions must reflect original ideas and voice

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

Residency

  • Open to citizens of any country
  • Must reside in the United States during the fellowship period
  • SSRC does not sponsor visas

Education

  • No formal degree requirements
  • Applicants may come from academic or non-academic backgrounds

Availability

  • Must commit to the full fellowship year

Restrictions

  • Full-time students are not eligible

Experience

  • Demonstrated engagement with issues at the intersection of technology and society

Selection Criteria

Applications are evaluated based on:

  1. Articulated Practice
    • Clear and coherent body of work
  2. Strength of Project
    • Relevance and originality
  3. Execution Capacity
    • Ability to complete the project
  4. Public Engagement
    • Evidence of sharing work with audiences
  5. Program Fit
    • Alignment with fellowship goals
  6. Collaboration
    • Willingness to engage with peers and contribute to the cohort

Application Timeline

  • Application Opens: April 27, 2026
  • Application Deadline: June 28, 2026 (11:59 p.m. EST)
  • Fellows Notified: November 2026
  • Fellowship Period: January – December 2027

Why You Should Apply

The Just Tech Fellowship stands out because it:

  • Provides substantial funding ($60,000)
  • Supports interdisciplinary innovation
  • Encourages real-world impact and public engagement
  • Connects fellows to a global network of experts
  • Offers flexibility and creative freedom

Click HERE to learn more and to apply

Contact Information

For inquiries, applicants can contact:

just-tech@ssrc.org

Discover more global Fellowship and Tech opportunities on OFY.

The post Just Tech Fellowship 2027 by Social Science Research Council (SSRC): $60,000 Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers, Creatives, and Practitioners in Technology & Society appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: One Million Coders Ghana: Transforming Africa’s Digital Future Through World-Class Tech Training
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
In an era defined by rapid technological advancement, digital skills are no longer optional—they are essential. The One Million Coders (OMC) Ghana initiative stands at the forefront of this transformation, offering a groundbreaking opportunity for individuals to gain cutting-edge tech expertise and become part of a thriving digital ecosystem. Spearheaded under the leadership of John […]

The post One Million Coders Ghana: Transforming Africa’s Digital Future Through World-Class Tech Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
In an era defined by rapid technological advancement, digital skills are no longer optional—they are essential. The One Million Coders (OMC) Ghana initiative stands at the forefront of this transformation, offering a groundbreaking opportunity for individuals to gain cutting-edge tech expertise and become part of a thriving digital ecosystem.

Spearheaded under the leadership of John Dramani Mahama, this ambitious national initiative is designed to empower one million Ghanaians with globally competitive digital skills, positioning Ghana as a leading technology hub in Africa.

What is One Million Coders Ghana?

The One Million Coders Ghana program is a large-scale digital capacity-building initiative aimed at equipping citizens with industry-relevant skills in technology and innovation. It combines:

  • High-quality training programs
  • Expert-led mentorship
  • Industry partnerships
  • Access to modern learning infrastructure

The goal is clear: build a future-ready workforce capable of driving economic growth through technology.

Why One Million Coders Matters

Digital transformation is reshaping industries across the globe. Ghana is taking proactive steps to ensure its citizens are not left behind.

Key Objectives of the Program

  1. Empower individuals with digital skills that meet global standards
  2. Promote innovation and entrepreneurship in the tech ecosystem
  3. Enhance employability in both local and international markets
  4. Strengthen Ghana’s digital economy

By participating, learners gain not just technical knowledge but also access to a vibrant community of innovators.

Available Courses and Learning Paths

The program offers a wide range of courses tailored to different career paths and skill levels.

1. Artificial Intelligence (AI)

  • AI Essentials
    • Duration: 10 hours
    • Level: Beginner
    • Learn to automate workflows, summarize data, and evaluate AI outputs
  • AI Professional
    • Duration: 10 hours
    • Level: Beginner
    • Integrate AI into real-world workflows and analyze complex data

2. Cloud Computing

  • AWS Cloud Practitioner
    • Duration: 70 hours
    • Level: Beginner
    • Learn cloud fundamentals, deployment, and security best practices

3. Software Development

  • Back-end Development (PHP)
    • Duration: 80 hours
    • Level: Intermediate
    • Focus on APIs, databases, and deployment
  • Back-end Development (Python)
    • Duration: 80 hours
    • Level: Intermediate
    • Build scalable backend systems
  • C++ Essentials & Advanced
    • Duration: 38–70 hours
    • Levels: Intermediate to Advanced
    • Develop high-performance and system-level software

4. Data Protection

  • Certified Data Protection Officer (CDPO)
    • Duration: 40 hours
    • Mode: In-person
    • Level: Intermediate

What You’ll Learn:

  • Monitoring compliance with data protection laws
  • Managing data risks and breaches
  • Responding to incidents
  • Ensuring regulatory compliance

5. Additional Learning Areas

  • Cybersecurity
  • Data Analytics
  • Digital Marketing
  • UI/UX Design
  • Networking
  • IT Support
  • Project Management
  • Digital Literacy

Program Benefits

Joining the One Million Coders initiative comes with significant advantages:

1. Access to World-Class Training

Courses are designed to meet global standards and industry demands.

2. Industry Partnerships

Collaborations with leading organizations such as:

  • Google
  • MTN
  • Telecel
  • Government institutions

3. Sponsored Learning Opportunities

Eligible participants may receive fully or partially sponsored training.

4. Practical, Career-Focused Skills

Programs are structured to prepare learners for real-world job roles.

5. Community and Networking

Gain access to a network of:

  • Tech professionals
  • Mentors
  • Fellow learners

Learning Format and Accessibility

  • Online and in-person options available
  • Training centers distributed across regions in Ghana
  • Flexible schedules to accommodate learners

The initiative ensures global quality education with local accessibility, making it inclusive and scalable.

How to Register

Getting started is simple:

  1. Visit the official One Million Coders platform
  2. Browse available programs
  3. Select your preferred course
  4. Complete the registration process

Once enrolled, you can immediately begin your journey toward becoming a tech professional.

REGISTER HERE

The Vision: Building Ghana’s Tech Future

The One Million Coders initiative is more than a training program—it is a national movement.

By empowering individuals with digital skills, Ghana is:

  • Creating job opportunities
  • Encouraging innovation
  • Attracting global investment
  • Strengthening its position as a tech leader in Africa

Participants are not just learners—they are future leaders shaping the digital economy.

Deadline

No specific deadline has been stated. However, early registration is strongly encouraged as spaces in certain programs may be limited.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global training Opportunities on OFY here.

The post One Million Coders Ghana: Transforming Africa’s Digital Future Through World-Class Tech Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GiveWell Researcher Job Opportunity 2026: Remote Global Role Paying Up to $162,400 to Drive High-Impact Global Change
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
If you are passionate about using evidence, research, and analytical thinking to improve lives worldwide, the GiveWell Researcher opportunity could be one of the most impactful remote jobs available in 2026. GiveWell is hiring exceptional Researchers to help direct hundreds of millions of dollars annually toward the world’s most cost-effective global health and poverty reduction […]

The post GiveWell Researcher Job Opportunity 2026: Remote Global Role Paying Up to $162,400 to Drive High-Impact Global Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about using evidence, research, and analytical thinking to improve lives worldwide, the GiveWell Researcher opportunity could be one of the most impactful remote jobs available in 2026. GiveWell is hiring exceptional Researchers to help direct hundreds of millions of dollars annually toward the world’s most cost-effective global health and poverty reduction programs.

This fully remote international role offers competitive compensation of up to $162,400 annually, outstanding benefits, global flexibility, and the chance to influence life-saving funding decisions at scale.

About GiveWell

GiveWell is a globally respected research organization focused on finding and funding the most effective opportunities in global health and development. Since 2007, GiveWell has directed more than $2.6 billion to high-impact programs and interventions, helping save over 340,000 lives.

The organization is trusted by tens of thousands of donors who rely on GiveWell’s transparent and evidence-based recommendations.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

Unlike traditional research roles, this position directly influences where major funding flows. Your analysis can shape decisions that affect millions of people worldwide.

As a Researcher, you will evaluate interventions, analyze evidence, build cost-effectiveness models, consult experts, and recommend where GiveWell should invest resources for the greatest measurable impact.

Salary and Compensation

GiveWell offers highly competitive location-based compensation:

  • $162,400 – New York City / San Francisco Bay Area
  • $147,300 – Other U.S. locations
  • International compensation aligned to comparable rates and local currency

Location

  • Fully Remote
  • Open to U.S. and International Applicants
  • Optional office locations in Oakland, Brooklyn, and London
  • Relocation support available in select cases
  • Visa sponsorship may be available for U.S.-based hires

Key Responsibilities

Successful applicants will work on some of the world’s most important development questions, including:

  • Reviewing evidence on global health and poverty interventions
  • Building cost-effectiveness models
  • Evaluating grant applications and funding requests
  • Advising on high-impact funding decisions
  • Consulting academics, policymakers, and field experts
  • Publishing transparent reports and findings
  • Solving complex research questions with creative methods

Example Areas of Research

Researchers may analyze programs such as:

  • Vaccine demand generation
  • Vitamin A supplementation
  • Seasonal malaria chemoprevention
  • Water quality interventions
  • Cash transfers
  • Rural infrastructure projects
  • Nutrition and livelihoods programs

Career Growth Opportunities

This role offers strong advancement pathways into:

  • Senior Researcher
  • Program Officer
  • Senior Program Officer
  • Team leadership and specialized grantmaking roles

Professionals can grow into owning entire funding portfolios or leading strategic research agendas.

Benefits

Employees receive a strong benefits package, including:

  • 100% funded health, dental, vision, and life insurance (U.S.)
  • Four weeks paid time off
  • 16 weeks fully paid parental leave
  • Retirement plan
  • Ergonomic home office support
  • Coworking memberships
  • Flexible working hours
  • International travel opportunities
  • Team retreats and conferences

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is ideal for candidates who:

  • Care deeply about global health and poverty reduction
  • Love rigorous research and evidence analysis
  • Think critically and independently
  • Communicate clearly
  • Enjoy solving difficult problems
  • Can prioritize high-value work
  • Thrive in remote, collaborative environments

Application Process

The hiring process includes:

  • Application review
  • Short application exercise
  • Two compensated work trials
  • Several short interviews

Applicants only need to submit a resume/CV and answers to application questions. No cover letter is required.

Application Deadline

There is currently no fixed deadline. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early application is strongly recommended.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply directly through GiveWell’s careers portal and prepare a strong CV highlighting research, analytical, quantitative, and impact-focused experience.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more internship opportunities, click here. 

Final Thoughts

The GiveWell Researcher position is more than a job—it is an opportunity to use your expertise to improve lives globally. With excellent pay, remote flexibility, and the chance to shape hundreds of millions in funding decisions, this is one of the most meaningful international research opportunities available right now.

If you are ready to combine career growth with measurable global impact, this is the role to pursue.

The post GiveWell Researcher Job Opportunity 2026: Remote Global Role Paying Up to $162,400 to Drive High-Impact Global Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AHSS Global Fellows 2026/27 at Queen’s University Belfast: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for International Academics
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
International academics seeking funded research opportunities in the UK can now apply for the prestigious AHSS Global Fellows Scheme 2026/27 at Queen’s University Belfast. This highly competitive fellowship supports scholars from around the world to spend between 4 and 12 weeks in Belfast, collaborating with leading researchers and advancing shared academic interests. Hosted by the […]

The post AHSS Global Fellows 2026/27 at Queen’s University Belfast: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for International Academics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
International academics seeking funded research opportunities in the UK can now apply for the prestigious AHSS Global Fellows Scheme 2026/27 at Queen’s University Belfast. This highly competitive fellowship supports scholars from around the world to spend between 4 and 12 weeks in Belfast, collaborating with leading researchers and advancing shared academic interests.

Hosted by the Faculty of Arts, Humanities and Social Sciences (AHSS), the program offers an exceptional opportunity to build global research networks, access world-class university resources, and contribute to impactful academic discussions.

What Is the AHSS Global Fellows Scheme?

The AHSS Global Fellows Scheme is designed to bring outstanding international scholars to Queen’s University Belfast for short-term academic visits. Fellows will work closely with colleagues in one of the Faculty’s four Schools while pursuing collaborative research projects and knowledge exchange activities.

This fellowship is open to academics at any career stage and across disciplines connected to the Faculty’s academic areas.

Fellowship Benefits

Successful applicants will receive a strong package of support, including:

  • Funding for visits lasting 4 to 12 weeks
  • Travel and accommodation funding up to £4,000
  • A courtesy desk within the host School
  • Access to university library resources
  • Opportunities to collaborate with respected academic staff
  • Platform to deliver a lecture, seminar, or workshop
  • International networking and research visibility

Longer stays may be possible through self-funded arrangements.

Available Host Schools

Applicants may be hosted within one of the Faculty’s four Schools:

  • Arts, English and Languages
  • History, Anthropology, Philosophy and Politics
  • Law
  • Social Sciences, Education and Social Work

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for:

  • University lecturers
  • Postdoctoral researchers
  • Professors
  • Independent scholars with institutional affiliations
  • Early-career and senior academics seeking international collaboration

Application Requirements

Before submitting an expression of interest, applicants must first secure sponsorship from a host School within the Faculty.

After selection, successful fellows will also need to complete the University’s formal Visiting Titles process.

Deadline

The deadline for applications for visits in the Autumn Semester of the 2026/27 academic year is:

Friday, 26 June 2026

How to Apply

Applicants should complete the official Expression of Interest form after receiving approval from a host School.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

Why Apply to Queen’s University Belfast?

Queen’s University Belfast is globally recognized for research excellence and interdisciplinary collaboration. Through this fellowship, scholars gain access to a vibrant academic community in one of the UK’s most historic and innovative cities.

If you are looking to expand your international research profile, develop partnerships, and experience academic life in Belfast, the AHSS Global Fellows Scheme 2026/27 is an outstanding opportunity.

The post AHSS Global Fellows 2026/27 at Queen’s University Belfast: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for International Academics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Turn Your Idea into a Scalable Business with the EDP Hope Fund Incubation Program 2026 | Fully Funded Global Opportunity for Social Entrepreneurs
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
If you are building a business idea that can transform communities while driving the fair energy transition, the EDP Hope Fund Incubation Program 2026 is your chance to grow faster, smarter, and with global support. This fully funded, five-month online incubation program is designed for early-stage entrepreneurs worldwide who want to create scalable and sustainable […]

The post Turn Your Idea into a Scalable Business with the EDP Hope Fund Incubation Program 2026 | Fully Funded Global Opportunity for Social Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are building a business idea that can transform communities while driving the fair energy transition, the EDP Hope Fund Incubation Program 2026 is your chance to grow faster, smarter, and with global support.

This fully funded, five-month online incubation program is designed for early-stage entrepreneurs worldwide who want to create scalable and sustainable businesses in clean energy, environmental sustainability, and inclusive innovation. Delivered by Bridge for Billions in partnership with EDP, the program offers mentorship, training, networking, and investment readiness support at no cost.

Whether you only have an idea, a minimum viable product (MVP), or an existing product still searching for a sustainable model, this program can help you validate your concept and build a clear growth roadmap.

Why Apply for the EDP Hope Fund Incubation Program 2026?

The global transition to fair and accessible energy requires bold entrepreneurs with practical solutions. This program is built to help innovators turn their ideas into real businesses that can scale impact.

Participants will gain access to:

1. Personalized Expert Mentorship

Work directly with industry professionals who will guide your progress, challenge your assumptions, and help refine your business strategy. Weekly mentorship sessions ensure accountability and continuous improvement.

2. Proven Business Incubation Methodology

Use a structured, step-by-step framework to validate your business model, identify customer needs, and create a scalable strategy.

3. Investment Readiness Training

Learn how to build financial forecasts, strengthen your go-to-market plan, and position your venture for future investors and partners.

4. Social Impact Measurement

Define measurable outcomes, create KPIs, and prove how your venture benefits communities and systems over time.

5. Global Community Access

Join a network of entrepreneurs, mentors, and professionals from around the world who are solving pressing social and environmental challenges.

What Will You Learn?

During the five-month incubation journey, selected founders will develop critical entrepreneurial skills, including:

  • Building a unique value proposition
  • Attracting and retaining customers
  • Pricing products and calculating costs
  • Marketing and communication strategies
  • Financial planning for the next three years
  • Measuring social impact
  • Scaling operations and expanding reach
  • Pitching your business effectively

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for founders whose projects focus on at least one of the following areas:

  • Access to clean energy
  • Energy efficiency
  • Green job creation
  • Re-skilling and up-skilling workers
  • Environmental preservation
  • Affordable and resilient solutions for underserved communities

Applicants can come from any country and any background. No age or educational restrictions apply.

Program Benefits

Selected participants will receive:

  • Free participation in a world-class incubation program
  • Weekly mentorship from experts
  • Access to 8 business development modules
  • Networking sessions and live workshops
  • Training in investment readiness and pitching
  • International visibility for your startup
  • Opportunity to pitch during the final Demo Day in November 2026

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: 30 April 2026
  • Selection Announcement: By 11 May 2026
  • Onboarding Session: 13 May 2026
  • Matching Phase: 15 May – 8 June 2026
  • Incubation Program: 8 June – 4 November 2026
  • Final Demo Day: November 2026

How to Apply

  1. Create an account on the official platform
  2. Complete all application sections carefully
  3. Review your responses
  4. Submit before the deadline

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

Why This Opportunity Matters

Many entrepreneurs have powerful ideas but lack the structure, mentorship, and network needed to grow. The EDP Hope Fund Incubation Program bridges that gap by helping founders turn purpose-driven ventures into sustainable businesses that can attract investment and deliver measurable impact.

If your startup can help shape a cleaner, fairer, and more inclusive future, this is the program to apply for now.

Final Reminder

Applications close on 30 April 2026. If you are ready to scale your impact and join a global ecosystem of innovators, submit your application before the deadline.

The post Turn Your Idea into a Scalable Business with the EDP Hope Fund Incubation Program 2026 | Fully Funded Global Opportunity for Social Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Turn Your Agribusiness Idea Into Impact: Win Funding, Mentorship & Tools with ABF’s e-Academy 2026 Call
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Agri-Business Facility for Africa, implemented by Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit, has launched an exciting opportunity for aspiring agripreneurs through its Agribusiness e-Academy Call for Submissions — Window 2 (March–May 2026). This initiative is designed to empower young entrepreneurs across Africa, the Caribbean, and the Pacific with the knowledge, tools, and exposure needed to […]

The post Turn Your Agribusiness Idea Into Impact: Win Funding, Mentorship & Tools with ABF’s e-Academy 2026 Call appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Agri-Business Facility for Africa, implemented by Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit, has launched an exciting opportunity for aspiring agripreneurs through its Agribusiness e-Academy Call for Submissions — Window 2 (March–May 2026).

This initiative is designed to empower young entrepreneurs across Africa, the Caribbean, and the Pacific with the knowledge, tools, and exposure needed to build investment-ready, climate-smart agribusinesses.

What This Opportunity Is All About

The Agribusiness e-Academy offers a free, structured learning pathway that helps participants transform raw business ideas into scalable, fundable ventures.

Participants who successfully complete the program and submit their ideas stand a chance to win:

  • €2,000 in funding
  • Mentorship support
  • A tablet or laptop

This initiative is part of a broader effort supported by the Organisation of African Caribbean and Pacific States, the European Union, and the German Government to strengthen agricultural value chains and improve economic resilience.

Why This Program Matters

Launching an agribusiness is challenging—especially when it comes to securing funding. Consider this:

  • Only 3 out of 10 early-stage businesses in Africa secure external funding
  • Investors demand:
    • Clear business models
    • Strong financial viability
    • Climate-smart strategies

This pathway addresses all three—at zero cost.

Who Can Apply

You are eligible if you:

  • Are 18 years or older
  • Are based in an OACPS member state
  • Have an agribusiness idea related to:
    • Cocoa
    • Maize
    • Cashew
    • Livestock (cattle, sheep, goats)

Step-by-Step Learning Pathway

The program is structured into a clear, progressive journey:

1. Business Model Canvas for Agribusinesses

  • Learn how real agribusinesses operate
  • Understand:
    • Cost structures
    • Pricing strategies
    • Revenue streams

2. Climate-Smart Agribusiness: Adaptation

  • Identify climate risks in your value chain
  • Learn practical:
    • Adaptation strategies
    • Risk management techniques

3. Creating an Investment-Ready Agribusiness

  • Evaluate your business idea
  • Test profitability
  • Prepare to pitch to investors

4. Build Your AgriCanvas

  • Develop your customized Business Model Canvas
  • Turn your concept into a structured, investment-ready plan

What You Can Win

After completing the learning pathway and submitting your AgriCanvas:

  • €2,000 funding
  • Mentorship opportunities
  • Digital tools (tablet/laptop)

How to Enter

Follow these steps carefully:

Step 1: Register

  • Create a free account on the atingi platform

Step 2: Complete Mandatory Courses

You must complete all three courses:

  • Business Model Canvas
  • Climate-Smart Agribusiness
  • Investment-Ready Agribusiness

Note: The third course unlocks only after completing the first two.

Step 3: Prepare Your AgriCanvas

  • Use the official template
  • Structure your idea clearly and logically

Step 4: Submit Your Idea

  • Enter your full AgriCanvas directly into the submission form
  • Provide:
    • Certificate ID (mandatory)
    • Climate-smart strategy
    • Revenue model
    • Capital requirements

Important: No file uploads—only form entries are accepted.

Submission Deadline

31 May 2026

Late or incomplete submissions (including missing certificate IDs) will not be accepted.

Selection & Evaluation Process

  • Ideas are reviewed by an independent international expert panel
  • Key evaluation criteria include:
    • Innovation
    • Financial viability
    • Climate resilience
    • Impact potential

Winners will be notified via email and announced around September 2026.

About the Organisers

The program is implemented by:

  • Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit
  • Agri-Business Facility for Africa

In collaboration with:

  • European Union (INTPA)
  • Organisation of African Caribbean and Pacific States
  • German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development

Together, they aim to strengthen agricultural value chains, improve food security, and create jobs and income opportunities.

Frequently Asked Highlights

Do I need to pay anything?

No. The entire program and courses are completely free.

Can I submit more than one idea?

No. Only one submission per applicant per window is allowed.

What if I’m not selected?

You can improve your idea and apply in future submission rounds.

Final Thoughts

This is more than a competition—it’s a launchpad for future agribusiness leaders. If you have an idea that can improve sustainability, empower communities, or transform agricultural systems, this is your opportunity to bring it to life.

For more information about this opportunity and to apply, click here.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post Turn Your Agribusiness Idea Into Impact: Win Funding, Mentorship & Tools with ABF’s e-Academy 2026 Call appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AU–EU Summit Model 2026 in Addis Ababa: Fully Funded Youth Diplomacy Simulation for African & European Leaders
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The AU–EU Summit Model 2026 is a prestigious youth diplomacy simulation designed to bring together young leaders from Africa and Europe to engage in high-level policy discussions, negotiations, and decision-making processes. Hosted at the African Union Commission Headquarters in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, this unique initiative provides a powerful platform for youth to actively participate in […]

The post AU–EU Summit Model 2026 in Addis Ababa: Fully Funded Youth Diplomacy Simulation for African & European Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The AU–EU Summit Model 2026 is a prestigious youth diplomacy simulation designed to bring together young leaders from Africa and Europe to engage in high-level policy discussions, negotiations, and decision-making processes. Hosted at the African Union Commission Headquarters in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, this unique initiative provides a powerful platform for youth to actively participate in shaping the future of Africa-Europe relations.

Organized through a collaboration between Model AU–Ethiopia, the European Union Delegation to the African Union, and the African Union Commission’s Women, Gender and Youth Directorate (WGYD), the Summit Model is a flagship youth engagement event taking place from May 12–13, 2026.

Strategically positioned between Europe Day (May 9) and Africa Day (May 25), the event symbolizes unity, cooperation, and the central role of youth in advancing the AU–EU partnership.

What is the AU–EU Summit Model?

The AU–EU Summit Model is a high-impact diplomatic simulation where participants assume roles such as:

  • Ministers
  • Heads of State
  • Ambassadors
  • Commissioners
  • Senior policy officials

Through these roles, participants simulate real-world AU–EU negotiations, gaining firsthand experience in:

  • Diplomatic dialogue
  • Policy formulation
  • Multilateral negotiations
  • Leadership and governance

The simulation culminates in the development of policy recommendations, which are shared with AU and EU stakeholders and representatives.

Core Focus Areas of the Summit

The 2026 AU–EU Summit Model will focus on key pillars of the AU–EU Partnership, including:

  1. Multilateralism
  2. Peace, Security, and Governance
  3. Green Transition and Economic Cooperation
  4. People-to-People Engagement
  5. Education and Youth Development

These themes reflect the broader strategic priorities outlined in:

  • Agenda 2063 (African Union)
  • AU–EU Joint Vision for 2030
  • EU Global Gateway Strategy

Why You Should Participate

The AU–EU Summit Model is not just a simulation—it is a transformative leadership experience. Participants will:

Engage with Global Peers

  • Collaborate with top young leaders from Africa and Europe

Gain Practical Diplomatic Experience

  • Understand how international organizations operate
  • Experience real-world policy-making dynamics

Develop Essential Skills

  • Public speaking
  • Negotiation
  • Leadership
  • Teamwork

Contribute to Policy Discussions

  • Provide youth-driven insights into continental and global issues

Deepen Knowledge of Global Frameworks

  • AU–EU Partnership
  • Agenda 2063
  • Global development strategies

Program Structure and Experience

The event will be held over two days (May 12–13, 2026) at the African Union Commission Headquarters.

Participants will:

  • Attend plenary sessions and committee meetings
  • Engage in structured debates and negotiations
  • Draft and present policy recommendations
  • Network with fellow delegates and organizers

Funding and Financial Support

The organizers will provide:

  • Program participation (fully covered)
  • Meals during the event

However, participants should note:

  • Transportation costs are NOT covered
  • Accommodation costs are NOT covered
  • Personal expenses are NOT covered

Participants are responsible for arranging and financing their travel and stay in Addis Ababa.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a national of an AU or EU member state
  • Be 17–30 years old
  • Be a:
    • Pre-college student
    • University student
    • Recent graduate
  • Be proficient in English or French
  • Demonstrate:
    • Passion for Agenda 2063
    • Interest in the AU–EU Partnership
    • Commitment to global development and diplomacy
  • Identify as a globally minded citizen committed to positive change

Application Process

To apply for the AU–EU Summit Model 2026:

  1. Complete the official Google Form registration
  2. Submit your application before the deadline
  3. Await confirmation after the application review process

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all details are accurate.

Application Deadline

Deadline: April 30, 2026
Time: 5:00 p.m. (Ethiopian Time)

Late applications will not be considered.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

The AU–EU Summit Model 2026 is a standout opportunity because it:

  • Provides direct exposure to AU and EU systems
  • Empowers youth to influence real policy discussions
  • Builds future diplomatic leaders
  • Strengthens Africa-Europe collaboration
  • Offers a prestigious platform for global engagement

Click Here to learn more about the AU–EU Summit Model 2026 in Addis Ababa.

Discover more global Conference opportunities on OFY Here.

The post AU–EU Summit Model 2026 in Addis Ababa: Fully Funded Youth Diplomacy Simulation for African & European Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ElevenLabs Ambassador Program 2026: How to Join, Benefits, Eligibility, and Opportunities for Creators and Community Builders
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The ElevenLabs Ambassador Program is a unique global opportunity designed for creators, innovators, and community leaders who are passionate about shaping the future of AI-powered voice technology. If you are already using ElevenLabs or interested in advancing your influence in the AI and content creation space, this program offers a powerful platform to grow your […]

The post ElevenLabs Ambassador Program 2026: How to Join, Benefits, Eligibility, and Opportunities for Creators and Community Builders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ElevenLabs Ambassador Program is a unique global opportunity designed for creators, innovators, and community leaders who are passionate about shaping the future of AI-powered voice technology. If you are already using ElevenLabs or interested in advancing your influence in the AI and content creation space, this program offers a powerful platform to grow your impact while gaining exclusive access to tools, funding, and opportunities.

ElevenLabs, a leading company in AI voice generation, is building a global network of ambassadors to help showcase what is possible with its cutting-edge technology. Through this program, selected individuals contribute by hosting events, creating content, and engaging communities around AI audio innovation.

What is the ElevenLabs Ambassador Program?

The ElevenLabs Ambassador Program is a global initiative that allows individuals to actively promote and expand the use of ElevenLabs tools within their communities or digital platforms. It is structured around two primary participation tracks: Event Hosting and Content Creation.

Participants in the program act as representatives of ElevenLabs, helping to educate others, demonstrate real-world use cases, and contribute feedback that shapes the development of the platform.

Ambassador Tracks: Choose Your Path

Event Host Track

As an Event Host, you will focus on building and engaging a local community. This includes organizing activities such as meetups, hackathons, workshops, demo nights, and listening sessions. ElevenLabs supports Event Hosts by providing funding, branding materials, and logistical resources. This track is ideal for individuals who enjoy leadership, networking, and community engagement.

Content Creator Track

Content Creators focus on producing educational and engaging materials that demonstrate how to use ElevenLabs tools. This includes tutorials, product demos, workflow breakdowns, and reviews. Content can be shared across platforms such as YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, LinkedIn, or other digital channels. This track is perfect for influencers, educators, and digital creators.

Applicants can choose one track or apply for both, depending on their interests and capabilities.

Benefits of Joining the ElevenLabs Ambassador Program

Participants gain access to a wide range of benefits designed to support growth and innovation:

  • Platform credits to use ElevenLabs tools
  • Early access to new features and product updates
  • Funding and resources for hosting events
  • Performance-based compensation for content creators
  • Direct access to the ElevenLabs team
  • Exclusive merchandise and branding materials
  • Invitations to special events and networking opportunities

Although the program is primarily community-driven and not a traditional salaried role, content creators can unlock paid opportunities depending on performance and audience engagement.

How to Contribute as an Ambassador

For Event Hosts:

  • Organize and manage local events
  • Represent ElevenLabs within their communities
  • Provide feedback to improve the platform

For Content Creators:

  • Develop high-quality tutorials and guides
  • Share practical workflows and insights
  • Demonstrate use cases and product features

Application Process

  1. Submit an application detailing your intended contributions
  2. Participate in a discovery phase if shortlisted
  3. Attend a brief introductory call with the ElevenLabs team
  4. Receive onboarding resources and begin contributing

Successful applicants will receive a welcome package, including onboarding guides, access to community platforms, and initial resources to get started.

Eligibility Criteria

  • Must be at least 18 years old
  • Must have an active ElevenLabs account
  • Demonstrate prior use of the platform
  • Have an existing audience, community, or clear contribution plan

Deadline

There is currently no fixed application deadline. Applications are open on a rolling basis, but interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible, as selection may be competitive.

Why You Should Apply

The ElevenLabs Ambassador Program is more than just an opportunity; it is a gateway into the rapidly growing field of AI-driven audio technology. Whether you are a student, creator, entrepreneur, or tech enthusiast, this program enables you to build authority in AI, expand your global network, gain hands-on experience, and contribute to shaping the future of voice technology.

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here. 

For more global opportunities, click here.

The post ElevenLabs Ambassador Program 2026: How to Join, Benefits, Eligibility, and Opportunities for Creators and Community Builders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Digital, AI and Innovation InInternship 2026 (UNDP): Global Remote Opportunity for Future Tech and Policy Leadersrs
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The United Nations Development Program (UNDP) has opened applications for its highly sought-after Digital, AI and Innovation Internship 2026, offering a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates worldwide to contribute to cutting-edge global development initiatives ; entirely from a home-based (remote) setting. This global internship sits at the intersection of technology, policy, and sustainable […]

The post Digital, AI and Innovation InInternship 2026 (UNDP): Global Remote Opportunity for Future Tech and Policy Leadersrs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Development Program (UNDP) has opened applications for its highly sought-after Digital, AI and Innovation Internship 2026, offering a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates worldwide to contribute to cutting-edge global development initiatives ; entirely from a home-based (remote) setting.

This global internship sits at the intersection of technology, policy, and sustainable development, making it one of the most impactful early-career opportunities for individuals passionate about artificial intelligence, data, innovation, and global problem-solving.

Why This Internship Matters

In today’s rapidly evolving world, global development is increasingly influenced by digital transformation, artificial intelligence, and data-driven decision-making. UNDP, operating in over 170 countries, plays a central role in tackling global challenges such as poverty, climate change, governance, and inequality.

Through its Digital, AI and Innovation (DAI) Hub, UNDP is driving inclusive digitalization and systems transformation. This internship gives participants a chance to contribute directly to these global efforts while gaining real-world experience in international development.

Internship Overview

  • Organization: United Nations Development Programme (UNDP)
  • Programme Type: Internship
  • Location: Home-Based (Remote)
  • Duration: 3 months
  • Schedule: Full-time (flexibility may apply)
  • Application Deadline: September 30, 2026 (rolling applications)
  • Start Date: Flexible (throughout 2026)
  • Language Requirement: Fluency in English

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning early submission significantly increases your chances of selection.

Key Areas You Can Work In

Interns will support UNDP’s work across multiple specialized tracks. Depending on your skills and interests, you may contribute to:

1. Systems Transformation & Innovation

Engage in research and development of innovative policies, conduct trend analysis, and help design scalable solutions to complex development challenges.

2. Programme Delivery & Capacity Building

Support the rollout of digital and AI initiatives globally, develop training materials, and assist in organizing workshops and webinars.

3. Data Policy & Governance

Work on data protection frameworks, digital rights, and governance policies shaping how countries use data responsibly.

4. Data Analytics & Insights

Analyze datasets, create dashboards, and translate complex data into actionable insights that inform decision-making.

5. Partnerships & Strategic Communications

Help identify partners, draft proposals, create communication materials, and support global engagement efforts.

6. Product Design & AI Tools Development

Collaborate with developers and teams to design digital platforms, apps, and AI tools that enhance UNDP’s work in different countries.

7. Research & Knowledge Management

Maintain knowledge platforms, produce research summaries, and support content dissemination across UNDP networks.

8. Process Efficiency & Resource Management

Track project milestones, improve operational processes, and develop internal systems for better efficiency.

What You Will Gain

This internship is not just about experience—it’s a gateway into global development and innovation ecosystems.

  • Exposure to international development work
  • Hands-on experience with AI, data, and digital transformation projects
  • Opportunity to collaborate with experts across multiple countries and disciplines
  • Development of research, analytical, and communication skills
  • A monthly stipend (amount varies by location and conditions)
  • Access to a global professional network within the United Nations system

Who Should Apply?

UNDP is looking for motivated, curious, and adaptable individuals who are eager to contribute to meaningful global work. Candidates must meet at least one of the following:

  • Currently enrolled in a graduate programme
  • In the final year of an undergraduate degree
  • Recently graduated (must start internship within one year of graduation)

Relevant experience or interest in digital innovation, AI, public policy, or development work is an added advantage.

Core Competencies Required

Successful candidates typically demonstrate:

  • Strong attention to detail and ability to meet deadlines
  • Innovative thinking and problem-solving skills
  • Willingness to learn and adapt quickly
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
  • Comfort working in fast-paced, multicultural environments

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet at least one of the following requirements:

Be enrolled in a graduate programme

Be in the final year of an undergraduate programme

Have recently graduated and be able to start within one year of graduation

Candidates with interest or experience in digital innovation, artificial intelligence, data analysis, or development work are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Process

Applicants are required to complete two steps:

Submit an application through the UNDP vacancy platform

Required Documents:

A current CV in English

A brief cover letter in English

Important Notes

Interns will receive a monthly stipend in accordance with UNDP policies. However, additional costs such as health insurance, internet, or other personal expenses are not covered. Applicants must have valid health insurance for the duration of the internship.

Due to the high volume of applications, only successful candidates will be contacted. UNDP strongly encourages individuals from underrepresented and marginalized communities to apply, in line with its commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion.

The UNDP Digital, AI and Innovation Internship 2026 offers a rare opportunity to gain hands-on experience at the forefront of global development and technological innovation. For students and graduates seeking to build a career in international development, AI, or digital transformation, this internship provides both the exposure and experience needed to stand out.

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early due to the rolling nature of the selection process.

Deadline

September 30, 2026 (Rolling applications : start early!)

APPLY HERE

For more information on this opportunity, click here.

For more global opportunities, click here

 

The post Digital, AI and Innovation InInternship 2026 (UNDP): Global Remote Opportunity for Future Tech and Policy Leadersrs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AMALI Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Shape Urban Governance in African Cities
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI) Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for early-career researchers passionate about urban development, governance, and data-driven transformation in African cities. This prestigious fellowship is hosted by the African Centre for Cities (ACC) at the University of Cape Town, in collaboration with Big Win Philanthropy and Bloomberg Philanthropies. […]

The post AMALI Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Shape Urban Governance in African Cities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Mayoral Leadership Initiative (AMALI) Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for early-career researchers passionate about urban development, governance, and data-driven transformation in African cities. This prestigious fellowship is hosted by the African Centre for Cities (ACC) at the University of Cape Town, in collaboration with Big Win Philanthropy and Bloomberg Philanthropies. It is designed to empower researchers to contribute meaningfully to policy, leadership, and sustainable urban growth across the continent.

The AMALI initiative focuses on strengthening the capacity of African city leaders by providing tailored support, evidence-based insights, and strategic guidance. A key component of this initiative is the Urban Governance Research Lab, which plays a central role in generating high-quality research, data, and policy recommendations for cities participating in AMALI.

The African Centre for Cities, established in 2007, is a leading interdisciplinary urban research institute located within the University of Cape Town’s Faculty of Engineering and the Built Environment. Its mission is to promote vibrant, democratic, and sustainable urban development across the global South through critical research and policy engagement.

This fellowship is ideal for scholars who are committed to advancing urban transformation in Africa through rigorous research, collaboration, and innovation.

Fellowship Overview and Structure

The AMALI Postdoctoral Research Fellowship offers two positions for qualified applicants. The fellowship is renewable for up to three years, subject to satisfactory progress. Fellows will be fully integrated into the Urban Governance Research Lab, where they will actively contribute to ongoing research and policy initiatives.

Successful candidates will engage in a range of activities, including collecting and analyzing qualitative and quantitative data on African cities, developing city profiles, and producing briefing notes on governance structures. Fellows will also conduct in-depth research on priority urban issues and collaborate on academic publications and other research outputs.

In addition, fellows are expected to participate in internal and public forums, engage with colleagues across the ACC’s research areas, and provide mentorship to postgraduate students. This creates a rich academic environment that fosters both personal and professional growth.

Research Focus Areas

Applicants must demonstrate expertise in at least one of the AMALI priority areas. These include water and sanitation, solid waste management, the informal sector, urban flooding, housing, urban markets, urban livelihoods, and urban health. These themes reflect the most pressing challenges facing African cities today and are central to the fellowship’s mission.

A strong understanding of urban governance and urban data in Africa is essential. Candidates with experience working across multiple African countries or with knowledge of different urban contexts will have a competitive advantage.

Eligibility Criteria and Requirements

Applicants must have completed a doctoral degree in a field related to urban studies within the past five years. Relevant disciplines include Urban Studies, Urban Planning, Urban Geography, Urban Health, and other related fields.

Candidates should demonstrate strong writing skills supported by academic publications, fluency in English, and a clear understanding of urban governance systems in Africa. While not mandatory, experience working in local government and proficiency in French are considered advantageous.

Applicants must not have previously held permanent full-time academic or professional positions. The fellowship is a full-time commitment, and selected candidates must be based at the ACC offices in Cape Town. Limited teaching or co-supervision responsibilities are allowed but must not exceed 12 hours per week.

Fellowship Benefits and Funding

The fellowship provides a generous annual stipend of R500,000, which increases by 5% each year. The funding is tax-exempt in accordance with SARS policies. However, it is important to note that the fellowship does not include additional benefits or allowances.

For non-South African applicants, obtaining a study visa may be required. This makes it essential for international candidates to plan their application and relocation process carefully.

Application Process

Interested applicants must submit a comprehensive application package via email. The required documents include a letter of application outlining research interests, expertise, and motivation; a two-page concept note addressing key challenges facing African cities and proposed solutions; a detailed CV with a list of publications; academic transcripts; and contact details of at least two academic referees.

All applications must be sent to Dr Amy Weimann, with the subject line clearly indicating “AMALI Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026”.

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is 15 May 2026.

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship is more than just a research opportunity. It is a platform to influence real-world urban policy, collaborate with leading experts, and contribute to the sustainable development of African cities. Fellows gain access to a powerful network of policymakers, researchers, and institutions committed to transforming urban spaces across the continent.

If you are an emerging scholar with a passion for urban transformation and a desire to make a lasting impact, the AMALI Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026 is an opportunity you should not miss.

Please submit your application to Dr Amy Weimann (amy.weimann@uct.ac.za).

For more information on this opportunity, click here .

For more global opportunities ,click here.

The post AMALI Postdoctoral Research Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity to Shape Urban Governance in African Cities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
